findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Password
findings.description: The product contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or for outbound communication to external components.
findings.evidence: RESOLVED: src/main/resources/application.properties and application-docker.properties previously contained hard-coded credentials (spring.datasource.username=photoalbum / spring.datasource.password=photoalbum). Fixed by replacing with environment variable references: spring.datasource.username=${DB_USERNAME:photoalbum} / spring.datasource.password=${DB_PASSWORD:photoalbum}.

findings.name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
findings.description: The product allows the upload or transfer of dangerous file types that are automatically processed within its environment.
findings.evidence: RESOLVED: PhotoServiceImpl.java previously validated file type using only the client-provided Content-Type header. Fixed by adding isAllowedImageContent() which validates actual file bytes against known image magic-byte signatures (JPEG: FF D8, PNG: 89504E47, GIF87a/GIF89a, WebP: RIFF..WEBP) independent of the Content-Type header.

findings.name: Unchecked Input for Loop Condition
findings.description: The product does not properly check inputs that are used for loop conditions, potentially leading to a denial of service or other consequences because of excessive looping.
findings.evidence: RESOLVED: HomeController.java uploadPhotos() previously iterated over a user-supplied files list with no upper-bound check. Fixed by injecting app.file-upload.max-files-per-upload (default 10) and rejecting requests that exceed this limit with a 400 Bad Request response.

findings.name: Improper Initialization
findings.description: The product does not initialize or incorrectly initializes a resource, which might leave the resource in an unexpected state when it is accessed or used.
findings.evidence: RESOLVED: PhotoServiceImpl.java previously allowed execution to continue with photoData=null when a non-IOException was silently swallowed, potentially persisting a Photo entity with null BLOB data. Fixed by adding an explicit null guard for photoData after the try-catch block that returns an error if photoData is null.

findings.name: Insufficient Logging
findings.description: When a security-critical event occurs, the product either does not record the event or omits important details about the event when logging it.
findings.evidence: PARTIALLY RESOLVED: PhotoServiceImpl.java empty-file validation branch (file.getSize() <= 0) now logs a warning via logger.warn(). Note: the application has no authentication layer, so authentication/authorization events are not logged — this remains an open concern if authentication is added in the future.

findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
findings.description: The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.
findings.evidence: RESOLVED: src/main/resources/application.properties (lines 11-12) and application-docker.properties (lines 3-4) previously contained hard-coded Oracle DB username and password. Fixed by replacing with environment variable references: spring.datasource.username=${DB_USERNAME:photoalbum} / spring.datasource.password=${DB_PASSWORD:photoalbum}.
